  "findings": [
    "Repetitive, sharp impact-like clicking and metallic clacking sounds indicating a severe localized defect on a bearing raceway or rolling element",
    "High-frequency transient ringing following each impact, characteristic of spalling or a cracked ring"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Schedule immediate shutdown of the conveyor drive to prevent catastrophic failure and secondary damage to the shaft and housing",
    "Perform vibration analysis to pinpoint the exact defect frequency (inner ring, outer ring, or ball/roller)",
    "Replace the bearing assembly and inspect the shaft and housing for wear or damage"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording reveals distinct, periodic, sharp metallic impacts superimposed on the normal background running noise. This signature is highly characteristic of a rolling element passing over a severe defect (such as a spall, crack, or deep pit) on the inner or outer raceway of a slow-turning bearing. The high risk of sudden seizure or shaft damage warrants a 'fail' decision and urgent replacement.",
  "references": [
    "ISO 10816 (Vibration severity standards)",
    "SKF Bearing Damage and Failure Analysis Guide",
    "ISO 15243 (Rolling bearings — Damage and failures)"
  ]
}
